verdict:
2026-07-01 23:12:02 PDT

The core shape is sound: file presence is the right DRY signal here, not a `workflow.json` flag, because the changelog file itself is the repo convention and a flag would be another drifting source. The retirement edit set is coherent: outside `reviews/`, the live dangling CHANGELOG doctrine is confined to `close/SKILL.md`, `BACKLOG.md`, and `.claude/workflow-protocol.md`; README/ARCHITECTURE do not need changelog edits. One procedural gap should be tightened before implementation.

findings.title: Make the zero-record close path explicit
findings.claim: The sketch gates the CHANGELOG bullet and rewrites the no-backlog clause, but the surrounding `/close` procedure currently relies on an unconditional record edit: step 5(b) always has something to commit because CHANGELOG is always written. Once CHANGELOG is optional, a repo with no `CHANGELOG.md` and a story with no tracked backlog item has no release-record file edits at all. If the step still says to commit the record changes unconditionally, Claude can hit a no-op commit failure or create an empty bookkeeping commit, which cuts against the new “merge commit + story file are the ship record” doctrine.
findings.alternative: Reword step 5(b) around a small declarative record set: update `CHANGELOG.md` only if present, update `BACKLOG.md` only if a tracked item is resolved, then `commit the record edits if any; if the set is empty, make no record commit and proceed to the re-gate/merge using the already-gated HEAD`. Keep step 5(c) as a gate against whatever HEAD will be merged.
findings.win: Eliminates a new empty-commit/error path introduced by optional changelogs, while preserving the no-new-config/no-third-record design and making the no-changelog/no-backlog case first-class instead of implicit.
